purpose: Honest REAL-USER-TIME wall-clock speedup benchmark for the v0.9.0 release: one RTX 5090 (single domain, fp64) vs 28-rank CPU-WRF v4.7.1 on the SAME workstation, same case / solution / forecast-length. Adds the speedup number to the v0.9.0 naive-agent README runnability gate and the README. CPU numbers are recovered from EXISTING run artifacts (NO new CPU runs). GPU numbers are PLACEHOLDERS to be filled by the PART-3 piggyback on the post-qke-fix validation runs.

branch_base.note: The GPU forecast path (gpuwrf.integration.daily_pipeline.execute_daily_pipeline) is on trunk-0.9.0; the d02-replay harness scripts/m7_l2_d02_replay.py + the d02_replay qke cold-start seed are on the qke-fix branch. The d03 1km replay scripts/d03_replay.py is on trunk.

metric_definition.wallclock_means: command-execution-to-finish: the real time a user waits from launching the forecast command to the last wrfout being written. NOT per-step compute, NOT a kernel microbenchmark.
metric_definition.why_compile_inclusive: The one-time JAX trace+XLA compile is part of what a user actually waits for on a cold launch, so the HONEST headline includes it. It amortizes over the forecast length (and over a daily operational cadence with a warm jax_cache). The steady-state (compile-excluded) number is reported SEPARATELY for context and is NEVER the headline.
metric_definition.comparison_basis: single RTX 5090 GPU (fp64, single domain) vs 28-rank CPU-WRF v4.7.1, SAME workstation (CPU on cores 4-31), same model time (per-forecast-hour), same case/date.
metric_definition.same_model_time_note: CPU-WRF runs dt=6s on d02 and dt=2s on d03; the GPU runs dt=10s (d02) / dt=3s (d03). The comparison is per identical FORECAST LENGTH (same model time, same number of output hours), which credits the GPU's larger stable timestep as a legitimate efficiency of the port. A strict dt-matched floor is reported separately.
metric_definition.conservatism_policy: Speedup is overclaim-prone. For the HEADLINE we use the most CONSERVATIVE (lowest) speedup: the FASTEST observed (least-contended) complete CPU run as the numerator-bound, and the compile-INCLUSIVE GPU wall as the denominator. We never cherry-pick the slowest CPU run or the steady-state GPU number for the headline.

honesty_apples_to_oranges_flags: GPU port runs ONE single domain (a 3 km d02 OR a 1 km d03) with PRESCRIBED lateral boundaries replayed from the corpus parent. It does NOT integrate the d01 9 km parent, and (for the 1 km case) it runs only ONE 1 km domain (d03 Tenerife 70x61), whereas CPU-WRF L3 runs THREE 1 km children (d03/d04/d05) plus d01+d02.

Therefore the ONLY honest CPU denominator is the STANDALONE per-domain cost (that domain's own solver time isolated from the nest), NOT the full multi-domain nest wall. We report the full-nest wall ONLY as labelled context, never as the speedup denominator. This avoids the retracted ~50-85x / 24x error (dividing one GPU d02 by the whole CPU nest).

The per-domain CPU cost is the domain's own 'Timing for main' solver time WITHIN the operational nest, isolated by domain id. It is NOT a clean max_dom=1 standalone CPU run (none exists). A true max_dom=1 standalone d02/d03 CPU run would TIGHTEN the band; it is flagged below as 'new run NOT launched, optional'.
fp64 on BOTH sides. The GPU number is fp64; fp32 gives ~0x on this launch/bandwidth-bound workload (see publish/runtime_optimization_analysis.md).
single GPU vs 28 CPU ranks on the same box: per-socket-vs-per-GPU, NOT per-watt or per-dollar.

CONTENTION: the CPU runs in the corpus were produced on a contended workstation (concurrent GPU + backfill jobs). Per-run wall-clock spans range ~2x across days. We use the FASTEST complete (least-contended) run for the CONSERVATIVE headline so contention overhead does not inflate the CPU denominator (which would inflate the speedup). The contended-day walls are reported as the upper band only.

cpu_provenance.wall_clock_derivation: TWO independent methods per run: (1) file-mtime span = mtime(rsl.out.0000) - mtime(freezeH2O.dat) [freezeH2O.dat is loaded by Thompson init immediately before time-stepping; rsl.out.0000 is closed at SUCCESS], giving the true command-to-finish wall; (2) sum / median of per-step 'Timing for main on domain N: X elapsed seconds' lines. On contended days method (2) sum EXCEEDS method (1) because the per-step wall timer counts time the rank-0 process was descheduled; the MEDIAN per-step is robust and is used for the per-domain standalone cost.
cpu_provenance.completeness_filter: Only runs with 'SUCCESS COMPLETE WRF' and the full expected per-domain step count are used. Incomplete/crashed runs (e.g. the 31 s/fc-hr L2 outlier, SUCCESS=False, 15694/57600 lines) are excluded.
cpu_provenance.live_backfill_avoided: The live 28-rank CPU-WRF backfill is writing /mnt/data/canairy_meteo/runs/wrf_l2_backfill_staging/20260513_..._20260604T062130Z (verified via /proc/<pid>/cwd). This lane read ONLY finished runs under wrf_l2/ and wrf_l3/ and did not touch the staging dir.

nested_9_3km.description: Canary Islands operational L2 nest: d01 9 km (94x60) + d02 3 km (160x67x45). GPU port runs the d02 3 km domain only (replay boundaries from d01). 72 h forecast.

20260528_18z_l2_72h.note: heavily contended day; full-nest wall ~2x the best run

ADOPTED_CPU_DENOMINATOR_d02_standalone.source: proofs/v090/cpu_wall_recover.py over the 4 least-contended complete L2 runs (reproducible). conservative=min, midpoint=median, realistic=max of the per-run d02 own-solver median s/fc-hr.
ADOPTED_CPU_DENOMINATOR_d02_standalone.basis: d02 own-solver median s/step x 600 steps/fc-hr (dt=6s), across the 4 least-contended complete runs (clean compute, isolated from d01). Slightly below proofs/perf/speedup_denominator.md adopted-83 because that doc used the more-contended 20260527/28 days; this lane deliberately picks the FASTEST CPU for a CONSERVATIVE (non-overclaiming) speedup. The CONSERVATIVE headline uses 64.6.
ADOPTED_CPU_DENOMINATOR_d02_standalone.comment: denominator_72h_wall = s_per_fc_hr * 72. The real_user_speedup divides this by the GPU command-to-finish wall for the SAME 72h forecast.

gpu.fill_via: PART-3 piggyback on the post-qke-fix d02-replay validation run (scripts/m7_l2_d02_replay.py --hours 72). See manager_steps_to_fill_gpu_numbers.

steady_state_reference_compile_excluded.value_s_per_fc_hr: 15.35 - 16.39 (PRIOR published warmed kernel number, NOT this benchmark's measurement)
steady_state_reference_compile_excluded.source: proofs/perf/segscan_24h.json + proofs/perf/warmed_timing.json (commit 177c734); publish/runtime_optimization_analysis.md headline 5.3x/7.84x is built on this compile-EXCLUDED number and is NOT the real-user-time headline.

gpu.precision_mode: gated_fp32 (ADR-007 ship: theta/u/v/qv fp32; mu/p/ph/w + acoustic/pressure fp64)
gpu.compile_honesty_note: TWO-PHASE COMPILE: the d02 forecast traces TWO large XLA graphs -- the no-radiation hour graph (hour 1, ~67s incl ~39s compile) AND the radiation-active hour graph (first hit in hour 2 = 435.6s, ~408s of which is the one-time RRTMG+physics+dycore compile). compile_overhead_s below (=hour1 - median(hour2..N)) only captures the hour-1 portion; the hour-2 radiation-graph compile (~408s) is the larger compile and is correctly EXCLUDED from steady_state (median is robust to the single hour-2 outlier) but is INCLUDED in gpu_wallclock_total_s (the honest command-to-finish). Total one-time compile across the run is ~447s of the 1166.5s wall. The compile-INCLUSIVE headline (1.33-1.59x) therefore already pays for both compiles; it amortizes much better over a 72h run or a warm jax_cache.

steady_state_speedup_compile_excluded.definition: CONTEXT ONLY (NOT the headline): cpu_s_per_fc_hr / gpu_steady_state_s_per_fc_hr (compile excluded).

dt_matched_floor_speedup.definition: strict dt-matched floor: real_user headline / dt_floor_factor (GPU forced to CPU dt).

results.note: Filled by the piggyback. real_user_speedup = cpu_72h_wall / gpu_wallclock_total_s (compile-inclusive). Use the CONSERVATIVE cpu denominator (68.5 s/fc-hr -> 4932 s/72h) for the headline lower bound; report the band to 85.9 s/fc-hr (6185 s/72h) as the realistic upper bound. dt_matched_floor divides the per-fc-hr speedup by 1.67 (GPU dt 10s vs CPU 6s).

single_1km.description: 1 km Tenerife domain. GPU port runs ONE 1 km d03 (94x76 staggered -> 93x75 mass, matches the corpus L3 d03 grid) replay forecast from d02 boundaries. CPU-WRF L3 runs d01+d02+d03+d04+d05 (THREE 1 km children). 24 h forecast. The honest CPU denominator is the d03 1 km domain's OWN-solver cost isolated from the L3 nest. GPU d03 grid == CPU d03 grid (94x76), so there is NO grid-extent mismatch for this case.

best_complete_run_least_contended.note: d01/d02 within the L3 nest are ~7x slower per step than in the L2 nest because the 5-domain nest is far more contended and the parents share the 28 cores with three 1km children. This is WHY the full-L3-nest wall is not a fair denominator. The d03 own-solver median (151.6 s/fc-hr) is the least-contention-sensitive per-domain figure (median over 43200 steps).

ADOPTED_CPU_DENOMINATOR_d03_standalone.source: proofs/v090/cpu_wall_recover.py over the 4 least-contended complete L3 runs (reproducible). conservative=min, midpoint=median, realistic=max of the per-run d03 own-solver median s/fc-hr.
ADOPTED_CPU_DENOMINATOR_d03_standalone.basis: d03 1km own-solver median s/step x 1800 steps/fc-hr (dt=2s), across the 4 least-contended complete L3 runs. CONSERVATIVE headline uses 110.3 (fastest observed CPU d03 median).
ADOPTED_CPU_DENOMINATOR_d03_standalone.STRONG_CAVEAT: This d03 cost is the domain's solver time WITHIN a contended 5-domain nest; it is NOT a clean max_dom=1 standalone 1km CPU run. The GPU d03 replay is single-domain. This is the best-available isolation from existing artifacts; a true max_dom=1 1km CPU run (flagged optional below) would be the cleanest denominator. The GPU d03 grid (93x75 mass = 94x76 staggered) MATCHES the corpus d03 grid, so there is NO grid-extent mismatch. The residual apples-to-oranges is the within-nest contention/subcycling of the CPU d03 (it is one of three 1km children sharing 28 cores), so the 1km speedup MUST be reported as INDICATIVE, not as a tight headline.

gpu.fill_via: PART-3 piggyback on the post-qke-fix d03 1km validation run (scripts/d03_replay.py --hours 24). See manager_steps_to_fill_gpu_numbers.
gpu.precision_mode: gated_fp32 attempted (ship mode); full fp64 is the documented-finite-but-throttled fallback
gpu.blocked_reason: d03 1km gated-fp32 went NONFINITE after forecast hour 1 (qke fp32 overflow; see proofs/v090/d03_1km_validation.json). No complete GPU wall-clock could be measured at ship precision. Full-fp64 d03 is finite but ~1:64-throttled (~16 min/0.3h) so a 24h fp64 timing also could not complete inside the burst. The 1km speedup is therefore UNMEASURED in this burst and remains a placeholder.

results.note: NOT FILLED: gated-fp32 d03 NaN (qke) before completion; fp64 d03 too throttled to time a 24h run. Fill this after the qke->fp64 promotion makes a ship-speed 1km run complete.

dt_matched_floor_note: Each case also reports a strict dt-matched floor: if the GPU were forced to the CPU timestep (d02: 6s vs 10s -> GPU x1.67 slower; d03: 2s vs 3s -> GPU x1.5 slower), the per-forecast-hour speedup divides by that factor. This is the hard floor that does NOT credit the GPU's larger stable timestep.

hardware.gpu: 1x NVIDIA RTX 5090 (GB202, compute capability 12.0), GDDR7 1.792 TB/s, fp64 = fp32/64
hardware.cpu: 28-rank WRF v4.7.1 on the same workstation, cores 4-31

manager_steps_to_fill_gpu_numbers: PRECONDITION: the qke-fix (worker/opus/v090-d02replay-qke-fix, 90fa7eb) is merged/landed and the d02-replay hour-1 stability is GREEN.

CASE nested_9_3km GPU number: run the d02 validation forecast that the manager already runs for the qke-confirm, but for the full forecast length matching the CPU denominator: `taskset -c 0-3 python3 scripts/m7_l2_d02_replay.py --run-id 20260523_18z_l2_72h_20260524T004313Z --hours 72 --proof-dir proofs/v090/speedup_d02`. (--hours 24 is acceptable as a faster proxy; the per-forecast-hour number is what feeds the speedup. The longer run amortizes compile better and is the more honest real-user number.) GPU on the free GPU; orchestration pinned to cores 0-3.
CASE single_1km GPU number: run `taskset -c 0-3 python3 scripts/d03_replay.py --hours 24 --proof-dir proofs/v090/speedup_d03` once the d03 1km path is stable post-qke-fix.
Each run writes proofs/v090/speedup_*/pipeline_run*.json (carrying wall_clock_total_s, wall_clock_per_hour_s [the per-hour list], wall_clock_per_forecast_hour_s) and wall_clock_*.json.

Then run the fill script: `taskset -c 0-3 python3 proofs/v090/fill_speedup_benchmark.py --d02-pipeline proofs/v090/speedup_d02/pipeline_run_l2_d02.json --d03-pipeline proofs/v090/speedup_d03/pipeline_run_*.json` which populates the gpu.* and results.* placeholders here (compile-inclusive total wall, compile_overhead = hour1_wall - median(hour2..N), steady_state from median(hour2..N)) and recomputes the speedups against the adopted CPU denominators.

OPTIONAL (tightens the band, NOT required for 0.9.0): a clean max_dom=1 standalone d02 and standalone d03 CPU-WRF run (cores 4-31, uncontended) would remove the within-nest contention residual from the CPU denominator. NOT launched by this lane.
new_cpu_runs_required: NO. All CPU wall-clocks were recovered from existing finished run artifacts (rsl.out.0000 per-step timing + file mtimes). A clean max_dom=1 standalone CPU run is OPTIONAL (would only tighten the band) and was NOT launched.
provenance_manifest: proofs/perf/speedup_denominator.md (d02 CPU denominator 83 clean / 123 realistic s/fc-hr, retraction of 50-85x)
scripts/m7_l2_d02_replay.py @ worker/opus/v090-d02replay-qke-fix (d02 GPU replay harness, emits wall_clock_*.json)
